note_data.title: Organic Chemistry
keyPoints: Organic chemistry is the branch of chemistry that deals with the study of compounds of carbon except carbonates, bicarbonates, carbides and carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide
Carbon has atomic number 6 and electronic configuration 1s² 2s² 2p² with four valence electrons in outermost shell
Carbon shows catenation property - the ability to form bonds with other carbon atoms to form chains and rings
Carbon forms covalent bonds due to small size and shows tetravalency by forming four covalent bonds
Hydrocarbons are compounds containing only carbon and hydrogen atoms
Alkanes have general formula CnH2n+2, alkenes have CnH2n, and alkynes have CnH2n-2
Saturated hydrocarbons contain only single bonds while unsaturated hydrocarbons contain double or triple bonds
Functional groups are specific arrangements of atoms that give characteristic properties to organic compounds
Homologous series is a group of compounds with same functional group and similar chemical properties

definitions.definition: Organic chemistry is the branch of chemistry that deals with the study of compounds of carbon except carbonates, bicarbonates, carbides and carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide
Catenation is the ability of carbon atoms to form bonds with other carbon atoms to form long chains and rings
Hydrocarbons are organic compounds which contain only carbon and hydrogen atoms
A functional group is an atom or group of atoms that gives characteristic properties to an organic compound
A homologous series is a group of compounds having the same functional group and similar chemical properties

importantQuestions.question: Define organic chemistry and give two examples of organic compounds.
importantQuestions.answer: Organic chemistry is the branch of chemistry that deals with the study of compounds of carbon except carbonates, bicarbonates, carbides and carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide. Examples: Methane (CH₄), Ethanol (C₂H₅OH)

importantQuestions.question: Write a detailed note on the unique properties of carbon that make it suitable for forming organic compounds.
importantQuestions.answer: Carbon has unique properties: (1) Small size allows strong covalent bond formation (2) Tetravalency - forms four covalent bonds (3) Catenation - ability to form chains and rings with other carbon atoms (4) Forms single, double and triple bonds (5) Electronic configuration 1s² 2s² 2p² with 4 valence electrons (6) Forms stable compounds due to strong C-C and C-H bonds (7) Can bond with many elements like H, O, N, S, halogens

mcqs.question: Which property of carbon allows it to form long chains?
The general formula for alkanes is:
Which of the following is NOT considered an organic compound?

commonMistakes: Students often include CO₂, CO, carbonates and bicarbonates as organic compounds
Confusing the general formulas of alkanes (CnH2n+2), alkenes (CnH2n) and alkynes (CnH2n-2)
Writing incorrect structural formulas without showing all four bonds of carbon
Not memorizing the exact textbook definition of organic chemistry word-for-word
Mixing up saturated and unsaturated hydrocarbons
examTips: This chapter usually has 1-2 MCQs and appears in short questions every year in BSEK papers
Always write the exact textbook definition of organic chemistry - marks are deducted for paraphrasing
Practice drawing structural formulas of first 4 alkanes - frequently asked
Memorize all general formulas and examples of each homologous series
Definition questions are very common - memorize all key terms exactly
